svg.title: Amazon Shopping HLD with Fallback Mechanisms
svg.desc: Complete Amazon shopping application HLD with fallback and crash recovery across all services — search, cart, pricing, inventory, orders, payments, delivery, observer fan-out, and all data stores.
svg.text: Search → Cart → Pricing → Order → Payment → Delivery + Full Crash Recovery

g.text: Auth (Cognito) · Rate limiting · TLS · A/B routing · Circuit breaker at edge
Static assets from S3/CDN — always available even if services are down
PriceRankingStrategy: cheapest first | PopularityRankingStrategy: best-seller rank
Elasticsearch: full-text, filters (brand, price, rating, prime-only, delivery day)
Personalisation: click / purchase history boosts via ML ranking layer
Cart persisted in Redis (user session) + DynamoDB (guest / cross-device sync)
Merge on login: server cart + local cart → union (higher qty wins)
Decorators composable — add Insurance, Subscription Discount, EMI surcharge independently
Inventory rollback on any failure (reserve → partial → release all)
onPlaced | onConfirmed | onShipped | onDelivered | onCancelled | onReturnInitiated

svg.text: — fallback zone 1: search / cart / pricing service failure —

g.text: Crash scenario A — search, cart or pricing service crashes

svg.text: — fallback zone 2: inventory reserve / order service crash —

g.text: Crash scenario B — inventory or order service fails

svg.text: — fallback zone 3: payment gateway chain failure —

g.text: Crash scenario C — payment gateway or wallet failure

svg.text: — fallback zone 4: observer / notification / recommendation failure —

g.text: Crash scenario D — fan-out observer failures

svg.text: — fallback zone 5: delivery / logistics service crash —

g.text: Crash scenario E — delivery routing or GPS tracking fails
Switch to secondary partner (Ekart → Blue Dart)
Tracking ID still generated — order not blocked
User gets ETA from stale DB estimate ±1 day
Email / SMS sent when live tracking resumes
Auto-downgrade to Prime (1–2 day) if slot missed
Kafka event triggers refund of same-day fee

svg.text: — 3-layer defence: Circuit Breaker → Retry + Backoff → Stale Cache —
— fallback zone 6: RDS Aurora / primary DB crash —

g.text: Crash scenario F — Aurora primary or read replica fails

svg.text: — crash recovery timeline: t=0 crash → full recovery —
t=0→t=40s: other AZ pods serve all traffic | CB OPEN → stale cache | Cart: local storage
Search: cached results | Pricing: last known | Orders: Kafka-queued | Payment: secondary PG
— fallback zone 7: Kafka (MSK) / event bus failure —

g.text: Crash scenario G — Kafka broker or consumer failure

svg.text: Kafka event + outbox replay on recovery
CDN static page + PNR lookup only

g.text: 1. Inventory reserve must be atomic — rollback all on any partial failure
PlaceOrderCommand reserves ALL items first. If any fail → ALL released. Per-productId sync prevents oversell.
2. Payment fallback chain — COD is the ultimate fallback, never fail the order
Primary PG → Secondary PG → Wallet → COD. CB on primary means 3 fails → OPEN → skip instantly to secondary.
3. Decorator for pricing — composable fee layers, never hardcoded totals
New fees wrap the existing chain without touching order logic. Adding insurance = new Decorator wrapping. Zero coupling.
4. CB state must survive crashes — persisted to Redis, not only in-memory
On graceful shutdown: CB state → Redis. On startup: CB hydrated from Redis. Prevents restarted pod hammering upstream.
5. Event sourcing — Kafka as truth, not the DB write
Crash after Kafka publish but before DB write: replay from offset → re-derive state. Outbox pattern = same transaction.

svg.text: Amazon: 66,000 orders/hr. Recovery SLA: <60s pod crash, <2min AZ failure. CB trip: 200ms.
3-layer defence (CB + Retry + Stale Cache) — user can always browse and checkout even when primary services degrade.
